breakdown: Agent noun from √su “to hear”: one who hears — a disciple who listens to and follows the teaching.

sāveti.gram: verb, 3rd person singular, causative of suṇāti (√su)
breakdown: Causative stem of √su “to hear”: to cause to hear.

sāyanhasamayaṁ.meaning: in the evening; at evening time
sāyanhasamayaṁ.gram: masculine noun, accusative singular (compound, used adverbially)

sīla.meaning: morality; moral conduct; the moral principles
breakdown: Root meaning “to practise, to be accustomed to”; sīla is settled, habitual good conduct.
sīla.goenka: Sīla — morality, moral conduct — is the first of the three trainings (sīla, samādhi, paññā) and the indispensable foundation of the path. Within the usual presentation of the Noble Eightfold Path, sīla comprises sammāvācā (right speech), sammākammanto (right action) and sammā-ājīvo (right livelihood); when the teaching is described in its triad of sīla, samādhi and paññā, the sīlakkhandha precedes the other two, stressing the fundamental importance of a wholesome ethical base. For lay practitioners this base is the five precepts, pañca sīla. In this tradition sīla is rendered “morality” or “moral conduct” — not mere “ethics” or external “rules”: it is the deliberate restraint of bodily and vocal action that makes concentration and insight possible.
sīla.note: divergence: the tradition avoids rendering sīla as “ethics”, “rules”, or bare “precepts” (pañca sīla = the five precepts).

sīlabbataṁ.meaning: morality and observances; rules and rituals
breakdown: Vow, religious observance, rite. (The double bb is sandhi doubling of v after sīla.)

sīlamevidha.meaning: morality indeed here; just morality in this world

sīlasampadāya.meaning: through the attainment of morality; by accomplishment in moral conduct

sīlavantassa.meaning: of the virtuous one; of one who keeps up morality

sīlavantānisaṁsā.meaning: the benefits of one who is virtuous; advantages of being established in sīla
sīlavantānisaṁsā.summary: This is a compound meaning “the benefits (ānisaṁsā) of the virtuous person (sīlavant)”.
breakdown: One possessing sīla, established in morality (vant is a secondary possessive suffix).
sīlavantānisaṁsā.note: uncertain: the VRI Burmese Chaṭṭha Saṅgāyana edition reads sīlavanttaānisaṃsā, which the lexicon judges to be a typo for sīlavant + ānisaṃsā.

sīlavato.meaning: of the virtuous one; of one observing morality
breakdown: Possessive suffix: “possessing”; sīlavato is an oblique form of sīlavant.
sīlavato.note: uncertain: the lexicon marks this form as nominative plural; sīlavato is more commonly the genitive/dative singular of sīlavant.

sīlavatūpapanno.meaning: endowed with morality and observances; furnished with virtue and good practice
breakdown: Endowed with, furnished with (past participle of upapajjati; the ū is lengthened in composition).

sīlavipattiyā.meaning: through failure in morality; by the loss of moral conduct

sīlādiguṇarahite.meaning: deprived of the qualities beginning with morality

sīlādiguṇasampanne.meaning: endowed with the qualities beginning with morality

sīsapāpaṇṇāni.meaning: sīsapā leaves; leaves of the siṁsapā tree

sīsapāvane.meaning: in the sīsapā forest; in the grove of siṁsapā trees

sītalaghanacchāyanīlavanasaṇḍamaṇḍitaṁ.meaning: adorned with dark-green forest groves of cool, deep shade
breakdown: Deep in shade, of dense shade (ghana “thick” + chāyā “shade”).

sītudakaghaṭasahassaṁ.meaning: a thousand pots of cool water
breakdown: Cold, cool (sīta + udaka contracts to sītudaka).

tabbipakkhato.meaning: from its opposite; on the side opposed to that
breakdown: That (the d assimilates, doubling the following consonant: tabbipakkha).

tadagge.meaning: since then; from that point onwards
breakdown: Beginning, point; locative agge: “from that beginning”.
The uposatha, the fortnightly observance day.

tadamināpetaṁ.meaning: and by this (means) this; thus in this way this too
breakdown: Also, and (elided to pe before etaṁ).

tadanuggahakāmataṁyeva.meaning: just the desire to help him; only out of the wish to show that compassion
tadanuggahakāmataṁyeva.gram: feminine noun, accusative singular + emphatic particle (compound)

tadatthavijānananti.meaning: the recognition of its meaning (with quotation marker ti)
tadatthavijānananti.gram: neuter noun (compound) + quotation particle, sandhi ṁ + ti > nti
breakdown: Sandhi of the accusative ending -ṁ with the quotation marker ti.

tadatthikasseva.meaning: just of one in need of that; only for one desirous of that

tadavasari.meaning: went down to that place; arrived there
breakdown: Went down to, arrived at (aorist of avasarati: ava “down” + √sar “to go”).
That (the d assimilates to the following d).

tadubhayameva.meaning: just both of these; both of them likewise

tadubhayavimuttaparisaṅkitañca.meaning: and suspected with regard to both of these, (not) freed from either
tadubhayavimuttaparisaṅkitañca.gram: adjective (compound) + conjunction ca, with sandhi ṁ + ca > ñca
breakdown: And (the preceding ṁ becomes ñ before c).

tadādāyakaupakkamasamuṭṭhāpikā.meaning: occasioned by the undertaking of the one who takes it at that instance
breakdown: Causing to arise, occasioning (feminine agent adjective).

tajjo.meaning: suchlike; corresponding to that; arising from that
breakdown: Adjectival suffix: “belonging to, born of” (tad + ya assimilates to tajja).

takkagovacchā.meaning: buttermilk cows; cows kept for buttermilk

takkahetu.meaning: on the ground of reasoning; by reason of logic

tama.note: uncertain: the lexicon lists the bare stem tama/tamo “darkness”; -tama is also a superlative suffix in other contexts.

tamaparāyaṇo.meaning: having darkness as destiny; bound for darkness
breakdown: Literally “finding one's support in”; having as destiny, ending in.

tamenaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion of two pronouns (taṁ + enaṁ), accusative

tamhi.note: divergence: the lexicon parses this as taṁ + hi “that + because/indeed”; tamhi is also the regular locative singular of the pronoun ta.

tamotamasuttaṁ.meaning: Tamotama Sutta, “From Darkness to Darkness” (a discourse title)
breakdown: Darkness — from one field of misery to another.

tanuttā.meaning: because of the reduction; the state of being diminished

breakdown: Here (a connecting t bridges the two words).

tassāyeva.meaning: of that very one; just of this
breakdown: Of that, of him (genitive singular of ta; lengthened in sandhi).

tasā.meaning: movable; trembling (of beings, in contrast to the still, thāvara)
breakdown: Root meaning “to tremble, to be frightened”.

tathattāya.meaning: to such a state; for the state of being thus
